title: Queensland commuters will face weeks of disruptions to their train services | ABC NEWS
description: South-east Queensland commuters set for weeks of disruptions with buses to replace trains as parts of the network shut down for major works. Services were back to normal on Thursday after hundreds were cancelled on Wednesday. But the war of words between the state government and unions is dragging on. #ABCNewsQld by Jack McKay Subscribe: http://ab.co/1svxLVE ABC NEWS provides around the clock coverage of news events as they break in Australia and abroad. title: Are insiders profiting from the Iran war? | ABC News Daily podcast
description: Oil and financial markets have been swinging wildly with every social media post from Donald Trump.    But a pattern of large, well-timed trades placed on commodity and prediction markets just minutes before major Iran war announcements has emerged.    It’s raising concerns that some investors could be acting on inside information.    Today, Andrew Verstein an expert on insider trading and law professor at UCLA on the money changing hands.   Featured:    Andrew Verstein, Professor of Law, and Faculty Co-Director of the Lowell Milken Institute for Business Law and Policy at UCLA.

title: Australia's jet fuel supply chain under threat from war in the Middle East | ABC NEWS
description: Australia imports most of its jet fuel from Asia, particularly China, Singapore and South Korea. But those countries rely on the Middle East for crude oil, which local refineries then convert into jet fuel, meaning they are also vulnerable. Experts warn future arrivals of jet fuel to Australia may not be so steadfast. title: Can Anthony Miller steady Westpac? | That's Business with Alan Kohler | ABC NEWS
description: More rate rises. A possible recession. And fresh pressure on households and business. So how is one of Australia's biggest banks preparing for what comes next? Anthony Miller took over Westpac after a bruising era for the bank, following a two-decade investment banking career with major institutions including Goldman Sachs and Deutsche Bank. He joins Alan Kohler on That's Business to talk mortgage stress, recession risk, cyber threats, AI, housing affordability, and how Westpac is trying to move on from years of scandal. title: What led to Pam Bondi's sacking as US attorney general? | ABC NEWS
description: Donald Trump has sacked his attorney general, Pam Bondi.  The long-time Trump loyalist had faced criticism over her handling of the Epstein files. In his announcement of her departure, Trump said Bondi would be "transitioning to a much needed and important new job in the private sector".  It's the second cabinet member he's removed in 2026 after Kristi Noem, who was sacked from her position as homeland security secretary in March. title: Trump warns Iran to "make a deal" as strikes intensify, urgent Hormuz talks held | ABC NEWS
description: A bridge in central Iran has been destroyed in an attack that killed eight people and injured almost 100 others, according to Iranian authorities. Donald Trump posted vision of the strike to social media, where he urged Iran to make a deal "before it is too late". Meanwhile, Australia has joined crisis talks aimed at re-opening the Strait of Hormuz as Iran's blockade of the crucial shipping lane continues to push up the price of oil.
